label: Game analysis / opinion piece
opening_rule: First sentence: state the specific argument being made about this game or phenomenon -- not background, not context, not what the game is.
closing_rule: Final paragraph: where the argument lands and why it matters beyond this specific game. One concrete claim, defended in 3-5 sentences.

sections.name: The argument
sections.purpose: Define the specific claim being made. Not 'this game explores X' but 'this game proves X at the expense of Y'. Sharp, falsifiable, defensible.

sections.name: Evidence
sections.purpose: Concrete examples from the game supporting the argument. Name specific mechanics, scenes, systems, design decisions. No vague references -- cite what happens and when.

sections.name: Counter-view
sections.purpose: Steelman the opposing position honestly. What would someone who disagrees say, and why is that position reasonable? Then explain why you reject it.

sections.name: Broader context
sections.purpose: Industry trends, historical precedent, comparable titles. What does this game's approach reveal about the genre, the studio, or the current market? Name specific comparisons.

sections.name: Conclusion
sections.purpose: Where the argument lands -- no hedging. State the position and what it means for players, developers, or the industry. Do not restate what was already argued. End on the implication, not the argument.
